jokes.text: Motels this size we're basically always face to face. In the same room.
jokes.setup: Johnny asking David to come speak face to face
jokes.punchline: David pointing out the absurdity given their cramped living situation
jokes.explanation: Great economy and character-specific observation about their reduced circumstances. David's dry delivery style fits perfectly.

jokes.text: Well, put them over the t-shirts then.
jokes.setup: Johnny complaining about David's clothes taking up closet space
jokes.punchline: David's completely impractical solution delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing David's disconnect from practical concerns, but not especially memorable.

jokes.text: Um... all that's missing is my middle name. / Then fill it in please. / Do I have to do that?
jokes.setup: Alexis needs to complete community service paperwork
jokes.punchline: Her reluctance to fill in something as basic as her middle name
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the middle name running gag. Shows Alexis's entitled laziness beautifully.

jokes.text: Do you not know my middle name? / Of course I do. I blessed you with it. / Okay, so um... then what name should I put down then?
jokes.setup: Moira claiming she knows Alexis's middle name
jokes.punchline: Alexis immediately calling her bluff by asking what it is
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the middle name bit. The pause before Moira's deflection is pure comedy gold.

jokes.text: Alexis... something rose.
jokes.setup: Moira trying to fake knowledge of Alexis's middle name
jokes.punchline: Her completely obvious guess based on their last name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - pretentious vocabulary masking complete ignorance. The ellipsis delivery is everything.

jokes.text: Wait. Alexis has a middle name? Why is this the first time hearing of this? Do... do I have a middle name?
jokes.setup: David overhearing the middle name discussion
jokes.punchline: His growing panic about his own potential middle name
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that broadens the family dysfunction. David's self-focused panic is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Now you come into the room.
jokes.setup: David finally entering after Johnny spent the scene calling for him
jokes.punchline: Johnny's dry acknowledgment of the timing
jokes.explanation: Nice button on the scene but relatively simple humor.

jokes.text: We sent them to the best boarding schools, we hired the best nannies... We did everything right.
jokes.setup: Johnny defending their parenting
jokes.punchline: Listing all the ways they avoided actually parenting
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary disguised as character blindness. Johnny's complete lack of self-awareness is the joke.

jokes.text: Um... I drove into the Prada Store on Rodeo Drive. In fairness, it did look a lot like, um, the entrance to a parking garage. And I was high at the time.
jokes.setup: Explaining her DUI charge
jokes.punchline: Each detail makes it progressively more absurd and privileged
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - each excuse makes it worse. The casual delivery of driving high into Prada is peak entitled obliviousness.

jokes.text: This isn't say yes to the dress, princess. Orange is the new orange.
jokes.setup: Alexis refusing the orange safety vest
jokes.punchline: Ronnie's reference to both the wedding show and prison show
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's both topical and character-appropriate for tough-talking Ronnie.

jokes.text: You try putting a cashmere sweater in the washing machine. It'll bite your wrist.
jokes.setup: Stevie questioning dry-clean-only clothes
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic personification of cashmere shrinkage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant David line - absurd imagery delivered with complete sincerity. The specificity of 'bite your wrist' elevates it.

jokes.text: It's one chromosome away from a crocheted blanket.
jokes.setup: David critiquing Stevie's sweater
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-scientific insult about the sweater's quality
jokes.explanation: Perfectly pretentious David insult using scientific terminology inappropriately. Shows his fashion snobbery perfectly.

jokes.text: Yeah, funky is a neon t-shirt you buy at an airport gift shop, next to a bejeweled iPhone case. This... this... is luxury.
jokes.setup: Stevie calling his clothes 'funky'
jokes.punchline: David's passionate defense with specific examples of actual funkiness
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing David's passion for fashion. The specific imagery makes it vivid and funny.

jokes.text: Speaking of unfit homes, why do you live in a barn? / What's wrong with living in a barn? / Mm... nothing, if you're a goat.
jokes.setup: Question about Mutt's living situation
jokes.punchline: Alexis's cutting comparison to farm animals
jokes.explanation: Clean setup-punchline with good delivery timing. Shows Alexis's snark without being mean-spirited.

jokes.text: I got these at a showroom in Paris. / I got these on clearance rack at Target. / $850. / 850 what? / Eight hundred- / Dollars?! / $850 dollars.
jokes.setup: Comparing their clothing sources
jokes.punchline: The absurd price difference and Stevie's shock
jokes.explanation: Great fish-out-of-water comedy. The stuttering escalation builds perfectly to Stevie's disbelief.

jokes.text: My car's worth less than your pants. / Well, I've seen your car and that makes sense to me.
jokes.setup: Stevie's observation about the price disparity
jokes.punchline: David's brutal but matter-of-fact agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect David response - obliviously cruel while being completely logical from his perspective.

jokes.text: My mom has this thing where half the time she thinks I'm her cousin Angela. And it's getting harder and harder to coordinate visitation times with my dad in prison.
jokes.setup: Johnny asking about getting along with parents
jokes.punchline: Twyla's casually delivered family tragedies
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy delivered with Twyla's characteristic cheerfulness. The escalation from dementia to prison is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Sadly, and most of the time, we have no interest in what's going on with you.
jokes.setup: Moira trying to be honest about their parenting
jokes.punchline: Her brutal honesty delivered as if it's helpful
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira - using elaborate language to deliver devastating honesty. The casual cruelty is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: No, Johnny, they had to guess which one was the truth. / Mm... which one was the lie. / It's just-it's just one lie. / You said two lies. / No, it's- / No, the game is 'two truths and a lie.' / It's truth. / That's the game. / Well, yeah, because babies play that at their birthday parties.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to explain a simple game
jokes.punchline: The entire family correcting him and his continued confusion
jokes.explanation: Great family dysfunction moment. The escalating confusion and Alexis's final dismissal is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Um, I'm miserable, drunk, and hate this game. So... here's a hint... Sadly, I'm not drunk.
jokes.setup: David demonstrating the game
jokes.punchline: Revealing that being drunk is the lie, making the other two true
jokes.explanation: Brilliant David moment - using the game format to express genuine misery with perfect deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Um... okay. My eyes are brown, I am basically sample-sized, and one time I escaped from a Thai drug lord's car trunk by bribing him with sex.
jokes.setup: Alexis playing the game
jokes.punchline: The increasingly outrageous statements ending in the drug lord story
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - casual delivery of an insane backstory. The escalation from physical features to international crime is masterful.

jokes.text: I clearly would never bribe anybody with sex.
jokes.setup: Alexis claiming the drug lord story was a joke
jokes.punchline: Her 'clarification' that makes it sound even worse
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that makes the situation worse. Alexis's attempt at damage control only highlights the problem.

jokes.text: Everyone can just calm down because Aroon was a lovely gentleman until he ran out of money.
jokes.setup: Trying to defend the drug lord
jokes.punchline: The casual way she names him and explains his turn to crime
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - giving the drug lord a name and sympathetic backstory makes it both more absurd and more believable for Alexis.

jokes.text: Mm-hmm. Guys like that will bone anything. / Well, not anything. / Stop. / 'Cause that would mean... / Stop. Stop. / That would include... / Stop it. / You. Which it doesn't. / Don't. / Ew.
jokes.setup: Alexis making a crude generalization about Mutt
jokes.punchline: David's growing horror as he realizes the implication about himself
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with David's mounting panic. The repetitive 'stop' builds tension nicely.

jokes.text: Oh, that is my favourite Liam Neeson movie.
jokes.setup: Johnny's dramatic recap of Alexis's drug lord story
jokes.punchline: Twyla's cheerful confusion of real life with 'Taken'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Twyla moment - innocent observation that highlights how insane their lives are. Great callback to the drug lord story.

jokes.text: Perhaps a new position would help?
jokes.setup: Jocelyn complaining about back pain from bed
jokes.punchline: Moira's innocent-sounding but loaded suggestion
jokes.explanation: Classic double entendre delivered with Moira's characteristic obliviousness to innuendo.

jokes.text: Couldn't put another thing in my mouth.
jokes.setup: Moira inviting Jocelyn to join them for food
jokes.punchline: Jocelyn's innocent but suggestive response
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre humor, saved by the context of the affair storyline.

jokes.text: It's too, you know... / Funky? / Sure. Let's go with that.
jokes.setup: Wayne struggling to describe David's clothes
jokes.punchline: David sarcastically supplying the word that offended him earlier
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the earlier 'funky' discussion. David's resigned acceptance is perfect character development.

jokes.text: This is French suede, with a vulcanized-rubber instep, okay? You've lost my trust and my business, so...
jokes.setup: Wayne dismissing David's expensive sneakers
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic departure over sneaker appreciation
jokes.explanation: Perfect David tantrum - technical fashion knowledge combined with complete emotional overreaction to rejection.

jokes.text: Look, I know you probably think that you're this handsome man around town who's like... Handsome, mysterious... And handsome and homeless-y
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to give Mutt advice
jokes.punchline: Her repetitive focus on his attractiveness and 'homeless-y' descriptor
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis moment showing her shallow priorities. The made-up word 'homeless-y' is perfectly her.

jokes.text: I have been in the middle of some very messy group scenarios
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to relate to Mutt's situation
jokes.punchline: Her casual reference to her own sexual adventures
jokes.explanation: Typical Alexis overshare delivered with complete casualness. The euphemistic language makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It's our son, Mutt. We're talking about our son.
jokes.setup: Roland and Jocelyn's conversation sounding like an affair
jokes.punchline: The reveal that they're discussing their son
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that resolves the affair tension. The timing of the reveal works well.

jokes.text: It's our son, Mutt. We're talking about our son.
jokes.setup: Seemingly romantic conversation between Roland and Jocelyn
jokes.punchline: Reveal that they're discussing their adult son, not an affair
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - the audience and other characters assume affair talk, but it's just parenting. Great structural surprise.

jokes.text: Good luck with birth control, right?
jokes.setup: Describing Mutt's anti-plastic lifestyle
jokes.punchline: Roland's crude observation about condoms
jokes.explanation: Typical crude Roland humor. In character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Good luck with birth control, right?
jokes.setup: Discussing Mutt's anti-plastic stance
jokes.punchline: Roland's inappropriate joke about his son's sex life
jokes.explanation: Typical Roland inappropriateness. Character-consistent crude humor about his own son.

jokes.text: Well, I just 'sold' a pair of sunglasses on here and now dieforfash87 is telling me she won't pay 'cause she thinks they're fake.
jokes.setup: David explaining his online selling troubles
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific username and his incredulous air quotes
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's frustration with his new reality. The username detail adds specificity.

jokes.text: Wayne huffs keyboard cleaner for a living.
jokes.setup: David defending his clothes' quality
jokes.punchline: His dismissive assumption about Wayne's drug use
jokes.explanation: Sharp David line showing his snobbery and specific knowledge of huffing. The casualness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: So why are you sneaking around with your mom anyway?
jokes.setup: Alexis confronting Mutt about his secret meetings
jokes.punchline: Her phrasing making it sound inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Decent setup for the parent-child dynamic reveal. Alexis's phrasing creates mild innuendo.

jokes.text: Um... yeah, because they always wanted a daughter with a record, who dropped out of college, so... Pretty much delivered on that one.
jokes.setup: Mutt asking if she's in sync with her parents
jokes.punchline: Alexis's sarcastic summary of her failures
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware Alexis moment. The casual delivery of her litany of failures is perfect.

jokes.text: So I thought you might be lonely without your clothes. So if this isn't too dirty for you, I could help take care of your stuff.
jokes.setup: Stevie making what sounds like a sexual proposition
jokes.punchline: The reveal that she's talking about clothes storage
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection that plays with David's and the audience's expectations. Stevie's deadpan delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Wait, you wanna have sex with me? / No. I don't think anybody has sex in here unless they're being paid for it.
jokes.setup: David's confused interpretation
jokes.punchline: Stevie's brutal assessment of the motel's clientele
jokes.explanation: Excellent Stevie line - deadpan delivery of dark humor about their circumstances. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: Emma. Your middle name is 'Emma.' / Is it? / I'm sorry! No, Hannah. / I'm embarrassed for you. / Elspeth. / No! / I think it's Elspeth. / It isn't! / I should know! / Wow.
jokes.setup: The ongoing middle name mystery
jokes.punchline: Johnny's increasingly desperate guesses and everyone's reactions
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the running gag. The escalating desperation and family shame is beautifully orchestrated.

jokes.text: I'm sorry! No, Hannah... I think it's Elspeth.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to guess Alexis's middle name
jokes.punchline: He keeps guessing wrong, getting more desperate and formal with each attempt
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the parental failure. Johnny getting more old-fashioned with each guess ('Elspeth') adds to the absurdity.
